title: malva althaea
intro: Malva althaea, also known as marshmallow, is a perennial plant native to Africa, Europe, and Asia, characterized by its tall stem and large, pink or white flowers. The roots and leaves of the plant have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties. Malva althaea is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Malva althaea include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
